headline: Musgrove and Pivetta are on the IL, the rotation is in freefall, and José Soriano ($2.9M arb) is the steal of the deadline.
executiveSummary: San Diego is 29-20 (1.5 GB NL West) with a .592 pace projecting to 96 wins, but the rotation is collapsing: Musgrove (D60), Pivetta (D15), Márquez (D15), Waldron (D15), and Hoeing (D60) have stripped the staff to Giolito, King, and Vásquez. SP holeScore of 11.17 is the most critical gap in the NL. With $53M of CBT headroom and Mason Miller as a blue-chip chip, the Padres must move in the next 72 hours. José Soriano — 2.69W at $2.9M arb1 — is the best controllable arm any rebuilder is selling; LAA at 17-33 has zero leverage left and every contender is circling.

todaysMove.action: Call LAA GM on José Soriano — open with Mason Miller as the centerpiece, close before a bidding war forces a premium
todaysMove.rationale: LAA is 17-33 with a closed window. Soriano earns $2.9M arb1 with ~4.8 surplusWAR over 3 years. Every NL contender with a rotation hole is mapping this same call; SDP's leverage erodes daily as buyers identify the same target. Acting now (before the July 31 deadline auction) caps the ask at Miller.
todaysMove.ifQuiet: If LAA stonewalls past 72h, shift immediately to MIA: Meyer + Phillips as a two-arm depth package costs only pre-arb bullpen pieces and fills two rotation slots.

keyFindings.title: Five SPs on IL — rotation depth is gone
keyFindings.detail: Musgrove (D60), Pivetta (D15), Márquez (D15), Waldron (D15), and Hoeing (D60) combine for $41.5M in unavailable salary. Active rotation is Giolito (2.08W), King (1.6W), Vásquez (1.18W), and negative-WAR depth. Without a deadline add, the 96-win pace collapses to an 85-win floor.

keyFindings.title: Soriano is a generational deadline value
keyFindings.detail: At 2.69W and $2.9M arb1, Soriano carries ~4.8 surplusWAR over 3 control years — the highest controllable surplus on any sub-.500 team's roster. LAA's 33-loss pace destroys their negotiating position.

keyFindings.title: Farm is thin — Miller is the only blue-chip chip
keyFindings.detail: SDP's surpluses array is empty in the warroom model. Mason Miller (arb1 $4M, 1.14W) is the single high-surplus controllable asset. Spend him once and spend him right — the SP crisis outranks bullpen depth.

keyFindings.title: $53M CBT headroom enables aggressive action
keyFindings.detail: At $190.7M committed vs. $244M CBT threshold, SDP can absorb Soriano ($2.0M prorated), Meyer ($0.7M), and Phillips ($0.5M) simultaneously without touching Tier 1. Full package flexibility exists.

keyFindings.title: Core veterans underperforming or expiring
keyFindings.detail: Tatis Jr. (-0.16W, $20.7M), Machado (0.13W, $25.1M), and Castellanos (-0.81W) are posting flat-to-negative WAR at $46.6M combined. The 2026 window is the last with this expensive core; clear deadweight in any package.

recommendations.action: Acquire José Soriano SP from LAA
recommendations.rationale: Soriano adds 2.69W annually at $2.9M arb1 — $2.0M prorated for 2026, keeping SDP comfortably Tier 0 at $192.7M. Against SP holeScore 11.17 and 5 arms on IL, this is the highest-leverage single move available. 96-win pace + fixed rotation ceiling = division title odds.

recommendations.action: Pair a MIA two-arm depth deal (Meyer + Phillips) as the parallel or fallback rotation fill
recommendations.rationale: Meyer (1.17W, $0.98M arb1) + Phillips (1.19W, $0.79M pre-arb) together equal Soriano's WAR at one-quarter the surplus cost — and MIA's teardown means SDP sends pre-arb bullpen pieces, not Miller. Total prorated addition: $1.3M. Do this even if Soriano lands as Slot 4-5 insurance.

recommendations.action: Hold Jackson Merrill — do not include in any package
recommendations.rationale: Merrill (age 23, pre-arb, 0.57W developing) is on +0.15W/yr aging trajectory with ~2.0 surplusWAR over 3 years. Trading him for a deadline arm is the worst risk-adjusted move in this system — he is the post-expiration core.

recommendations.action: Use Nick Castellanos as free salary filler in every package
recommendations.rationale: Castellanos is -0.81W at $780K (league-minimum-level) and blocked by Tatis Jr. at RF. His prorated cost to any recipient is $0.5M — essentially a free roster spot. Include him in all packages to fill counterparty positional holes without surrendering real surplus.

recommendations.action: If LAA and MIA both stall, pivot to Davis Martin (CHW) as the fallback ace
recommendations.rationale: Martin (2.58W pre-arb $799K) has the highest 3yr surplusWAR of any fallback target (~5.7). CHW's rebuild means their leverage is structurally weak. A Miller + Castellanos package delivers comparable fills to what LAA would extract.

likelihoodDrivers: LAA at 17-33 has no leverage — Soriano's $2.9M arb is wasted in a rebuild
Miller fills LAA RP hole (holeScore 3.17) with an arb1 proven closer
Castellanos fills LAA LF/RF depth at essentially zero prorated cost ($0.5M)
blockers: Miller is SDP's active closer — bullpen depth must be assessed post-trade
LAA may prefer multiple pre-arb prospects over one proven veteran arm
SDP net surplusWar +3.4 = buyer-premium deal; LAA will test other buyers first
proposedPackages.framing: You're 16 games under .500 — Soriano's surplus belongs on a contender; we give you Miller as a proven closer today, clear Castellanos' dead weight, and your rebuild gets the better of the 'pen deal.

likelihoodDrivers: MIA full teardown — Meyer and Phillips are more valuable deployed on a contender
Castellanos fills MIA RF hole (holeScore 2.66) at $0.5M prorated — near-free for them
Rodriguez is active pre-arb arm that fits MIA's rebuild timeline
SDP stays Tier 0 ($192.0M prorated) — no cap escalator risk
blockers: MIA may demand a higher-tier prospect to monetize Meyer's $0.98M arb1 contract
Rodriguez is an active SDP bullpen piece — weakens 'pen depth
proposedPackages.framing: You're clearing for 2028 — swap two sub-$1M arms for a proven active reliever and bridge RF who costs you $1.3M prorated; we fill your RF hole and you accelerate the prospect timeline.

likelihoodDrivers: CHW rebuild renders Martin more valuable to a contender — their internal leverage is near zero
Miller fills CHW RP hole (holeScore 2.34) with an arb1 closer they can develop
Castellanos bridges CHW RF hole at league-minimum cost
blockers: CHW may prefer a prospect haul over a proven-veteran package
Martin's pre-arb status means CHW knows his ceiling — reluctant to move without a multi-piece return
Package is SDP's fallback only — LAA and MIA are higher-probability at better surplus value
proposedPackages.framing: Martin is your best chip in a rebuild context; we deliver a proven closer (Miller) and bridge RF today — you move a pre-arb arm in the last year before his arb raises hit.

targetBoard.likelyAsk: Elite arb1 reliever (Mason Miller tier) + controllable OF or IF depth

counterpartyLeverage.trigger: 17-33 record eliminates any realistic 2026 playoff path; Soriano's $2.9M arb is dead surplus on a rebuilder
counterpartyLeverage.openWith: You're not contending — Soriano's value is maximized in San Diego's rotation right now; Miller fills your RP hole (holeScore 3.17) and we clear Castellanos off your hands at no cap cost.

counterpartyLeverage.trigger: 22-28 full teardown; Meyer and Phillips sub-$1M contracts are peak-trade-value assets MIA should monetize before arb raises hit
counterpartyLeverage.openWith: You're building for 2028 — convert Meyer and Phillips into an active arb reliever and bridge RF who fills your two biggest holes right now.

counterpartyLeverage.trigger: Rebuilding roster; Martin is their highest-surplus arm and every contender is mapping this call — CHW will shop him if the ask is right
counterpartyLeverage.openWith: Martin is your best trade chip and we'll give you a proven closer (Miller) plus bridge RF — cleaner than a prospect package, and he's arb1 before the raises hit.

counterpartyLeverage.trigger: 25-24 but SP holeScore 10.76 critical — OAK likely retains Civale and Springs for internal use rather than strengthening a division rival
counterpartyLeverage.openWith: If Springs or Civale is truly available, we'll absorb the full prorated salary for a depth prospect in return.

capImpact.note: Trivial Tier-0 add; SDP at $192.7M committed after acquisition — $51.3M under CBT
Sub-$1M prorated; adding both Meyer + Phillips still keeps SDP at $193.4M Tier 0
Pre-arb cost; combined Meyer + Phillips acquisition is $1.2M prorated — immaterial to CBT
Sending Miller frees $2.79M prorated; RP depth (Wandy Peralta, Jason Adam, Estrada) must cover closing duties post-trade

contentionTimeline.note: Peak window — Tatis, Machado, Bogaerts all expiring; rotation crisis is the only obstacle
Post-expiration payroll cliff; Merrill + farm cohort must carry the offense
Transition year — controllable core (Merrill, Vásquez, acquired arms) at peak; depends on 2026 farm investment
Unknown direction — farm depth (score 30) limits long-term floor without aggressive retooling
Speculative; hinges entirely on post-2026 rebuild decisions
Long-range tail — insufficient data beyond 2028 core commitments
